subject: [PATCH] cxl/acpi: Fix CXL_ACPI and CXL_PMEM Kconfig tristate mismatch

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Identified a potential issue with forced runtime error path being decided at compile time, but is open to investigating further and potentially auto-converting config settings.

reviews.author: Keith Busch
reviews.summary: Clarified that the forced runtime error path was intended to be decided at compile time, and is open to alternative approaches.
reviews.raw_body:
On Thu, Mar 05, 2026 at 05:56:06PM -0500, Gregory Price wrote:
> > Anyway, my proposal here should force demotion to "m" for CXL_ACPI if
> > PMEM is also "m", where your proposal should force a failure path at
> > compile time if ACPI=y and PMEM=m. I really don't know if there's any
> > preference at all, I also just wanted the kernel build to complete
> > successfuly. :)
> 
> my proposal allows compilation but fails during probe because the symbol
> is IS_REACHABLE()=false, but yeah, i have no idea what we want here.

Indeed, I didn't mean to suggest your solution may not build. I only
mean the forced runtime error path was decided at compile time. And I'm
totally okay with doing that if it's preferred over forcing module
demotion.

2026-03-05.patch_summary: The patch aims to fix a Kconfig tristate mismatch between CXL_ACPI and CXL_PMEM, but the discussion revolves around whether to auto-convert config settings or handle it at compile time.
